lines: The lock gives hard. The iron door swings on a cavity that smells of cold metal and old paper.

Inside: a handful of relic rounds the warden never logged, a saint token rubbed faceless and kept for luck, and his own seal ring, the diocese sun-cross worn smooth from turning locks. A last note is folded under the ring.

'They sent me a boy who would not stop singing it. That the Host had simply stopped. That no saint died to halt the Devil, that the Pontifex knows the difference and lets us kneel anyway. I collared him with the sick and set him to the litany. He calcified in the cellar with the rest, still singing, when the cold came down.'
The hand presses harder for the last of it. 'He may have been right. I kept his silence and my seal, and I am going to walk out of this house and never say a word of it. You can carry it instead.'
